---
description: "Rules for producing authentically human writing by avoiding telltale AI-generated text patterns"
---

# AI Writing Avoidance — Auto-Loaded Rules

Full vocabulary ban list, structural rules, tone corrections with examples → see ai-writing-avoidance skill.

## Quick Audit Pattern

Before finalizing any document:

1. Ctrl+F for banned vocabulary (delve, myriad, plethora, tapestry, beacon, landscape, realm, paradigm)
2. Check first paragraph for preambles ("In this document, we will explore...")
3. Check last paragraph for restating conclusions
4. Count bullet lists (max 3 per page)
5. Verify at least one specific example exists
6. Confirm document has a point of view

## Policy Document Rules

- Lead with what people must DO
- Use role names, not "stakeholders"
- Include real incident references where appropriate
- State consequences directly
- Keep paragraphs under 4 sentences

## Red Flag Thresholds

| AI Tells Found | Action Required |
|----------------|-----------------|
| 0-2 | Minor polish |
| 3-5 | Section rewrite |
| 6+ | Full document revision |

- Pair with `documentation-quality-assurance` for systematic reviews
- Use before any external publication
- Apply to email templates and form letters
- Required for customer-facing policy documents
